raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> I received a call from my Citibank Mastercard about suspected fraud. They listed several transactions in different parts of the city for large amounts. I let them know that all of the charges were fraudulent as I'd been home at the time and was unfamiliar with these neighborhoods and stores. The total charges are {$3000.00}. 

On XX/XX/year>, I received a letter from Citibank denying the disputes and saying that because the card 's EMV chip had been used and I had confirmed that the card was in my possession, they refuse to consider this as fraud. 

The spending spree that the thieves went on lasted for 3 days, during which I was in my neighborhood, at home. Given that they called me suspecting fraud, which I confirmed, this refusal is ludicrous.
